building_row.management_company: Greystar California, Inc. dba Greystar Corp.; Kilroy Realty asset management/owner context
building_row.open_questions: Verify that the exact unit is not exposed to plaza events, Sunset/Gower activity, or adjacent construction. Confirm package-handling flow, resident parking assignment, and whether any security changes followed the recent anecdotal Google/Birdeye complaint. Confirm approval timing and the exact total monthly stack for the target unit.
building_row.notes: Strong official amenity and fee disclosure, and the essentials match well: in-unit washer/dryer, dishwasher, AC, garage parking, pet-friendly policy, concierge, secured elevators, and pool/deck. Quiet is the main tradeoff: the building sits on an active Hollywood campus, Kilroy describes outdoor plaza events including live music, and the site also includes public parking. Structural noise risk looks better than average because the tower was built as a Type I concrete high-rise. Management signal is mixed: Apartments.com shows a strong 4.8/5 blended score from 13 renter reviews, but a 2024 resident petition objected to valet removal and Birdeye surfaces lower Google review sentiment plus a serious but unverified anecdotal security complaint. That conflict lowers management confidence and keeps this in tour-first territory rather than a clean lock.

packet_markdown:
# Columbia Square Living building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: columbia-square-living-1550-n-el-centro-ave
- Address: 1550 N El Centro Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://www.columbiasquareliving.com/
- Management company: Greystar California, Inc. dba Greystar Corp.; Kilroy Realty asset management/owner context
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-25

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (medium, evidence count 3)
- Management: 3 (medium, evidence count 4)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 5)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 5
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: low
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: medium
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official Greystar listing shows AC, dishwasher, pet-friendly policy, washer/dryer, garage parking, rooftop pool/deck, concierge, secured elevators, and a private resident entrance. Source: Greystar property page (https://www.greystar.com/columbia-square-living-los-angeles-ca/p_17235). Note: Highlights include Air Conditioning, Dishwasher, Garage, Pet Friendly, Pool, Washer/Dryer; community amenities include Rooftop Pool & Deck, Resident Rooftop Lounge, Lobby & Dedicated Concierge, Card Reader Secured Elevators, and Private Resident Entrance.
- [official] Greystar discloses several recurring required monthly charges, including pest control, billing admin fee, and SCEP fee, but some utility and lease-cost items remain usage-based or lease-specific. Source: Greystar pricing and costs disclosure (https://www.greystar.com/columbia-square-living-los-angeles-ca/p_17235). Note: Greystar says Total Monthly Leasing Price includes base rent, mandatory monthly costs, and any optional add-ons; the visible table lists Pest Control Services at $3.00/month, Utility Billing Administrative Fee at $4.38/month, and SCEP Fee at $2.83/month, while several utility lines are usage-based.
- [review] Apartments.com shows strong headline resident sentiment and detailed operational disclosures, but resident review depth is still modest. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/columbia-square-living-los-angeles-ca/2v16sgc/). Note: Apartments.com shows a 4.8/5 blended score with 13 renter reviews and lists Property Manager on Site, Controlled Access, Package Service, Concierge, Doorman, and 24 Hour Access.
- [official] The larger Columbia Square campus hosts outdoor events and includes public parking, which raises location-noise exposure for some stacks even if the tower itself is upscale. Source: Kilroy Columbia Square on-site amenities page (https://columbiasquare.kilroyrealty.com/property-overview/on-site-amenities/). Note: Kilroy says the outdoor plaza hosts events including live music and a holiday tree lighting ceremony, and the site has a public 5-level parking garage with valet parking and EV charging.
- [review] A resident petition in 2024 complained that valet removal undermined one of the key service promises residents valued. Source: Change.org resident petition (https://www.change.org/p/reinstate-valet-service-at-columbia-square-living). Note: The petition says the removal of valet service undermined one of the principal reasons residents chose the building.
- [review] Google-review sentiment visible through Birdeye is materially weaker than Apartments.com and includes a serious recent security allegation, but the allegation is not independently confirmed by a higher-tier source. Source: Birdeye Google review aggregation (https://reviews.birdeye.com/columbia-square-living-166314006746336). Note: Birdeye shows a 3.9/5 rating across 104 Google reviews and surfaces a recent complaint alleging an active-shooter incident involving a maintenance employee and poor building response. This is important but remains anecdotal without a corroborating official report in the retrieved set.
- [official] The project was constructed as a Type I concrete high-rise, which is a favorable structural signal for airborne-noise isolation versus lighter wood-frame construction. Source: Webcor project page (https://www.webcor.com/projects/columbia-square-living?f5d8a238_page=2). Note: Webcor describes Columbia Square Living as a ground-up, Type I concrete high-end apartment project with 22 levels above grade.

## Open questions
- Verify that the exact unit is not exposed to plaza events, Sunset/Gower activity, or adjacent construction.
- Confirm package-handling flow, resident parking assignment, and whether any security changes followed the recent anecdotal Google/Birdeye complaint.
- Confirm approval timing and the exact total monthly stack for the target unit.

## Notes
- Strong fit on essentials and useful extras, but quiet is exposure-dependent because of the Hollywood campus setting and public/event activity.
- Management signal is mixed rather than broken: strong official operations and Apartments.com score, offset by a resident valet petition and weaker Google/Birdeye sentiment.
- Best treated as a serious tour candidate if the specific stack is quiet and operational questions are answered clearly.
